steps.text: Ensure the conference supports CDC's mission.
Verify the scientific meeting meets the Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences.
Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) or his/her designee if funding is included.
Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) or his/her designee if funding is not included.
Ensure sponsorship is consistent with CDC's policy on Guidance for Collaboration with the Private Sector.
Ensure the conference is fully accessible to the public as required by the ADAAG.
Provide CDC with documentation of a plan with a timeline for substantive involvement in, and development of, the content of the meeting.
Provide a written process of how presentations or abstracts are to be reviewed and criteria that will be used for selecting presentations.
Agree to abide by CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences, when applicable.

Establish a written process for approving conference funding which includes information on how proposals for conference funding will be solicited, elements required in a proposal, and a process for review and approval of proposals by the CC/CO/NC.
Document review and approval of conference funding by the CC/CO/NC including a copy of the proposal, how the review was conducted, criteria for review, and whether funding was approved.

Obtain approval by the Director, or his/her designee, of the CC/CO/NC(s) providing the funds for each grant, cooperative agreement, interagency agreement, contract, or purchase order awarded for conference support.
Ensure CDC approval if CDC is to be identified as a sponsor of the event when portions of general program funds are used to support conferences.
Include specified language in all program announcements for CDC sponsorship of nonfederal conferences when funds are to be awarded.
Submit a formal request in writing to the CCs/COs/NCs or the Office of the Director (OD), CDC.
Provide the organization's mission.
Provide the purpose of the conference.
Provide the organizer's demonstrated capacity to plan and conduct such a meeting.
Provide proposed content and format.
Provide intended audience.
Provide information on other cosponsors, their mission, and their role in the conference.
The CC/CO/NC Director, or his/her designee, reviews and approves or disapproves all requests.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating disapproval of the request if not sponsoring.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval if interested in sponsoring.
CDC must participate in the development and approval of portions of the conference for which CDC's name is used.
Review the content of the full agenda, press events, promotional materials, speaker selection, site selection, and reports or proceedings.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating final approval or disapproval of sponsorship.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor after final approval is provided.

The Director, or his/her designee, of the CC/CO/NC(s) providing funds or approving CDC sponsorship of a conference must assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG prior to approving any request or issuing any obligation documents.
Obtain written certification from management of the proposed meeting facility stating that facilities, goods and services are fully ADA compliant.
Ensure that the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist and keeps it on file with other conference documentation.
Maintain a copy of all written correspondence regarding conferences that CDC is asked to sponsor.
Ensure records include documentation required as per Section 4H.
Account for each party’s programmatic and financial responsibilities and activities.
Describe separately any discrete portion of an event sponsored by CDC or a non-Federal entity.
Make agreements and records concerning the amounts, sources, and uses of funds available to the public upon request.
Assign a specific person responsibility for maintaining records.
Submit a quarterly line listing of all conferences sponsored or disapproved to the OD, CDC.
Maintain records of the quarterly listings and make them available to the CC/CO/NCs upon request.

preconditions.text: CDC shall not co-sponsor an event with an entity that will not make information concerning funding publicly available.

constraints.text: All conferences sponsored by CDC shall be held in facilities that are fully accessible to the public as required by American with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).
